Start here. | | [0] + [1] No sign-up path | **Fix — one ticket** | Same  ·  Do first: **Determine whether [4] is the 0-cards bug.** Before treating the broken export UI and the 0-cards observation as separate work streams, check whether cards are being generated in the system but failing at the export/delivery step. Pull server logs for the live runs. If the export pathway is broken** (full transcript under `autotest/reports/council/`)

## 🔁 Regressed (a closed defect came back — fix first)

_These were previously fixed or auto-closed and have RE-APPEARED. A recurrence after closure is the highest-signal finding here._

### [HIGH] Cards lack round/heat label — prelim and final swims appear as impossible duplicates · `4fe01d0e3bb7`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `output`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-01T21:43:18+00:00` · last `2026-06-10T20:40:41+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** Each card identifies the round (e.g. Prelim, Final, Heat 3) so that two legitimate first-place finishes by the same swimmer in different rounds are distinguishable
- **Actual:** Cards show swimmer, event, medal, and time but no round or heat designation; a prelim gold and a final gold by the same swimmer are rendered as duplicate impossible medals with no context
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman analysis: '27.42 / 27.24 split is almost certainly prelims and finals, not a deduplication error — both are genuine first-place finishes in their respective rounds'; source file MISM-2024-RESULTS.PDF must be read before any fix is written
```

### [HIGH] Per-swimmer data lookup failure is the likely shared root cause of issues [0] and [1] · `a7bf971f553b`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `semantic:output/high`
- **Seen:** 3× · first `2026-06-01T11:48:25+00:00` · last `2026-06-11T20:32:34+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** Issues [0] (wrong caption body) and [1] (confidence=medium on unambiguous gold medals) investigated as a single defect in the per-swimmer data pipeline rather than two independent bugs
- **Actual:** Filed as two separate issues; fixing the per-swimmer data lookup for [0] will almost certainly resolve the confidence downgrade in [1] as a side effect, meaning the true issue count is smaller than it appears
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
Chairman verdict: 'If the per-swimmer data lookup is failing and falling back to meet-aggregate stats, that same failure explains why individual achievements can't be verified with high confidence, producing medium across the board. These aren't two independent bugs.'
```

### [HIGH] Pipeline data-ingestion step not triggered — zero runs loaded upstream of card generation · `b6f86c8a8de9`

- **Category:** `council:blind_spot`
- **Where:** `functional/pipeline`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-02T14:40:53+00:00` · last `2026-06-13T09:26:35+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Expected:** Flow loads runs associated with the selected club before attempting card generation, then reports parsed_swim_count and our_swim_count
- **Actual:** Flow completed with zero runs ever inspected, meaning data was never loaded — the pipeline did not run and produce nothing, it never reached any data at all
- **Evidence:**

```
Surfaced by the LLM Council peer-review (a blind spot the single judges missed):
"the failure in [0] is probably upstream of card generation, not inside it. The flow token says inspected-0-runs — the pipeline never reached any data, it didn't run and produce nothing. 'Zero runs inspected' points to a data-association or configuration failure: no meets linked to the club, a missing club association, or a silent auth degradation."
```

### [HIGH] After approval the queue shows 'ready to export' but no export button or next step exists · `deeefa4ee496`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `review / approve flow`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-12T10:25:27+00:00` · last `2026-06-14T04:26:33+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** A clear 'Export', 'Download pack', or 'Copy to Stories' button directly following approval so the volunteer can actually get their content
- **Actual:** The approved state surfaces only a status label and a reassurance statement — the volunteer who just approved their first card has nowhere to go to retrieve the content they came for
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
Tom Davies — PB 100m Free · ready to export
NOTHING LEAVES THE QUEUE WITHOUT YOUR APPROVAL.
```

### [MEDIUM] Two separate login triggers in the same nav bar · `76a8133ead76`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `navigation / sign-up`
- **Seen:** 2× · first `2026-06-12T20:20:34+00:00` · last `2026-06-14T09:46:08+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** One clear entry point that routes new users to sign-up and returning users to login
- **Actual:** Navigation bar lists both 'SIGN IN' and 'LOG IN' as separate items with no explanation of the difference
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
SIGN IN
LOG IN
```

### [LOW] 'DEVELOPER' and 'ONLINE' in the main nav confuse a non-technical volunteer · `e65c724bdc88`

- **Category:** `semantic:user_brain`
- **Where:** `homepage / nav`
- **Seen:** 5× · first `2026-06-13T14:11:28+00:00` · last `2026-06-15T04:50:38+00:00`
- **Repro:**
  1. Reproduce the flow (result: live:inspected-0-runs-no-content) and inspect the user_brain aspect described above
- **Expected:** All top-level nav items are recognisable to a volunteer (Home, Create, Templates, Pricing, etc.)
- **Actual:** 'DEVELOPER' and 'ONLINE' sit in the same nav bar as HOME and CREATE. A volunteer doesn't know if they need to visit 'DEVELOPER' to make their upload work, or what 'ONLINE' signals.
- **Evidence:**

```
AI judge (user_brain, confidence high):
DEVELOPER
ONLINE
```

### [HIGH] a11y: <html> element must have a lang attribute (html-has-lang) · `bea9ae54f585`

- **Category:** `a11y`
- **Where:** `/manifest.webmanifest`
- **Seen:** 23× · first `2026-06-10T20:40:41+00:00` · last `2026-06-13T19:47:40+00:00` · ⚠️ not reproduced in latest run
- **Suspected source:** `axe:html-has-lang`
- **Repro:**
  1. Open /manifest.webmanifest
  2. Run axe-core; rule 'html-has-lang' flags 1 node(s)
- **Expected:** The page meets the WCAG rule axe-core checks
- **Actual:** axe-core 'html-has-lang' violation (serious) on 1 element(s)
- **Evidence:**

```
axe-core rule: html-has-lang
help: <html> element must have a lang attribute
helpUrl: https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.12/html-has-lang?application=axeAPI
example targets:
  html
```

### [HIGH] a11y: Select element must have an accessible name (select-name) · `3d7b7df57b3f`

- **Category:** `a11y`
- **Where:** `/organisation`
- **Seen:** 34× · first `2026-06-02T14:40:53+00:00` · last `2026-06-15T04:50:38+00:00`
- **Suspected source:** `axe:select-name`
- **Repro:**
  1. Open /organisation
  2. Run axe-core; rule 'select-name' flags 2 node(s)
- **Expected:** The page meets the WCAG rule axe-core checks
- **Actual:** axe-core 'select-name' violation (critical) on 2 element(s)
- **Evidence:**

```
axe-core rule: select-name
help: Select element must have an accessible name
helpUrl: https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.12/select-name?application=axeAPI
example targets:
  select[name="org_type"]
  select[name="language"]
```

## ⏳ Pending confirmation (subjective — not yet a bug)

_AI/judge findings seen too few times to open. The fixer IGNORES these; each sweep they recur bumps the count, and at `AUTOTEST_CONFIRM_SWEEPS` they become open bugs. One-shot noise decays out instead._

- [HIGH] No 'Sign Up' CTA — only 'Sign in' and 'Set up my organisation' · `b2df5a0d2c97` (semantic:user_brain) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-14T19:47:38+00:00` · not in latest run
- [HIGH] Primary hero CTA says 'Sign in' — sends new users into the wrong flow · `57643969abcf` (semantic:user_brain) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-15T04:50:38+00:00`
- [HIGH] Review demo dead-ends at 'ready to export' with no export action visible · `daea4edc45df` (semantic:user_brain) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-15T04:50:38+00:00`
- [MEDIUM] 'ONLINE' rendered as a nav item — looks like a leaked status indicator · `eea2717618ce` (semantic:user_brain) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-14T19:47:38+00:00` · not in latest run
- [MEDIUM] '020 TOTAL RUNS' — leading zero looks like a display bug · `8a1558448437` (semantic:user_brain) — confirmed 0× since `2026-06-14T19:47:38+00:00` · not in latest run

- Navigation failed: crawl:/static/fonts/bigshoulders-latin-normal-800.woff2 · `a12292408770` → PR https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/210 (merged 2026-06-01T04:51:26Z)
- Meet-wide average stat templated into every individual swimmer card · `0fc5bf4cd758` → PR https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/211 (merged 2026-06-01T23:16:41Z)
- Duplicate cards for the same swimmer, event, and medal with only a time difference — no explanation · `f51fbb0c9424` → PR https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/219 (merged 2026-06-02T11:48:17Z)
- Meet level shown as 'NATIONAL' but the meet name says 'Regional' — contradictory metadata a volunteer will distrust · `202e6f197299` → PR https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/218 (merged 2026-06-02T11:42:00Z)
- 'REVIEWED 3/3 = 100%' contradicts 54 cards still in queue — false completion signal · `a31b1bda242d` → PR https://github.com/elijahkendrick04/MediaHub/pull/212 (merged 2026-06-01T23:16:50Z)

</details>

<details><summary>🗂️ Verified-fixed — retired with evidence (14)</summary>

- a11y: Documents must have <title> element to aid in navigation (document-title) · `33893e777a30` — verified via `48e913f` (tests/test_autotest_a11y.py::test_a11y_audit_gated_to_html_content_types); False positive. axe-core's document-title rule fired on the synthetic viewer Chromium renders for the non-HTML route /healthz/search; this is not a real WCAG document — the route serves JS/JSON, not an HTML page. Eliminated at source by the finder-precision fix (PR #443): the a11y audit is now gated to HTML responses. Loop-authored fix PR #433 closed as a false-positive fix. — by elijahkendrick04 (operator approval) via Claude Code — finder-precision fix PR #443 at 2026-06-13T19:39:44+00:00
- a11y: Documents must have <title> element to aid in navigation (document-title) · `ac6f77e15778` — verified via `48e913f` (tests/test_autotest_a11y.py::test_a11y_audit_gated_to_html_content_types); False positive. axe-core's document-title rule fired on the synthetic viewer Chromium renders for the non-HTML route /manifest.webmanifest; this is not a real WCAG document — the route serves JS/JSON, not an HTML page. - Upload and processing completed successfully but yielded no content cards. · `e883e6f0bbc7` — verified via `5c86706 (#196, product honest empty state) + this PR (finder precision: functional rubric v2)` (tests/test_autotest_empty_state_precision.py (8 passed); tests/test_review_empty_state_club_match.py (3 passed)); FALSE-POSITIVE: '/review 200 + 0 cards' is the CORRECT, explained club-mismatch empty state, not a contradiction. The functional judge mis-flagged it because content_summary omitted the swim-match counts; the fix gives the judge those counts so it no longer false-positives. — by LLM Council (finder-precision verdict, autotest/reports/council/) + human merge of this harness PR at 2026-05-31T16:50:03+00:00
